title: Minimum Arrows to Burst All Balloons
description:
Given There are balloons represented by intervals [x_start, x_end]. An arrow shot at position x bursts all balloons with x_start ≤ x ≤ x_end. Return the minimum number of arrows to burst all balloons.

    

Constraints:
    - 0 ≤ n ≤ 10^5
- -2·10^9 ≤ x_start ≤ x_end ≤ 2·10^9
explanation:
Intuition
Shoot arrows at the right edge of the leftmost finishing balloon. Once an arrow is placed, skip all balloons overlapping that point, and repeat. Sorting by end coordinate ensures each arrow covers as many as possible.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Sort intervals by end ascending
2. Main Algorithm: Initialize arrows=0 and pos=-inf; for each interval if start>pos, shoot at end and set pos=end
3. Return Result: Return arrows

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n log n)
- Space Complexity: O(1) extra

Key Insights
- End-sorted greedy maximizes coverage per arrow
- Strict start>pos handles closed intervals

Common Pitfalls
- Using start sort is not optimal
- If you use ≥ instead of > you may overshoot coverage
hints: Place arrow at the current right endpoint
